title: My experience with AI LLM code generation
author: Seetaram N T
text:
My experience with AI LLM code generation
I have been exploring AI code generation tools right from the day one chatGPT was available for the public use. I used to try mostly C, C++, Python, Java and Javascript/Typescript code generation and in the intial days, the tools used to produce incomplete code and abruptly stop. So it was not of much use then but was useful in getting some good ideas, concepts etc required to implement the code manually. Over the time, the AI LLM tools improved drastically and could produce pretty decent code for a specific problem statement. I have mainly explored chatGPT (different versions), DeepSeek and Google gemini for code generation. Off late i am using more of Gemini which looks good for me.
I have been developing cross platform apps since past few years using ionic framework. Ionic is a framework which supports code once and build for multiple targets type of architecture where the same code can be built for windows web, Android and iPhone. I am mainly focusing on ionic Angular apps even though ionic supports Vue and ReactJS. Unlike writing code in C, c++, java, Python etc, Ionic app development is more challenging in terms of lots of interdependencies in terms of framework, cli, plugins, npm’s, JDK, Android studio etc. So having a compatible development setup is very critical before even starting a new project. Secondly most of these dependencies keep getting updated continously and hence we need to keep updating the stuff on timely basis. There is big community of a node packages to support Ionic and hence it is pretty hard to keep track of these npm’s and their documentation etc. So all in all, unless you are an experienced developer in this domain, it is bit cumbersome to put everything in proper place to get the working code and build.
Recently i tried to build a complete app from scratch using AI tools and here is my experience in how it went doing it. The goal was not to write a single line of code nor to fix any issues manually but to use AI LLM to do everything. The app i created was an Ionic angular app for my montly expense managment. Here are the project requirements for this app.
- The app should be Ionic angular supporting windows, Android and Iphone. I generally build it and use in Android as Android development environment is free to use and Android doesn’t pose restrictions to install third party APK.
- The app should support cloud based storage to allow multiple people to login simultaneously and be able to log expenses.
- It should allow creating/deleting and manging multiple expense records.
- The user interface should have options to open the existing record or create new and once created, it should have options to log the expenses data which mainly contains fields like, spender name, amount, description and category of expense etc.
- Each record should have edit/delete button to modify or delete records.
- The spender name should support an Autocomplete feature where the existing names should pop up for selection once the input character match the starting few characters of the stored names.
- The UI should show all the expense records in order. It should show category wise spending as on date, the settlement summary should have total spending, spending of each person and if expenses are divided among the people, it should also show the settlement data as to who has to pay whom.
- The app should have option to delete the expense record with password protection to ensure deliberate attempt to delete data.
- The app should implement the a social sharing option to share the expesne report in email, whatsApp etc.
- The app should support option to set budgeting for each category of expenses and also to monitor the exepenes category wise. If it exceeds the set limit, it should notify and also show it in yellow in the UI.
- The app should support AI enabled feature of auto category prompt where it prompts the category of expense based on the description details entered!
- The app should implement an Auto budget setting AI feature based on the previous spending data.
- Once the end of month is reached, it will create a new expense with the name of the month and year as the filename so that, the user can start logging expenses in the new record.
- Implemented OCR to read bills to log expenses automatically but it is not that useful!
- App should support adding some fixed monthly expenses automatically as soon as the new expense record is created. This helps to avoid recurring expenses manually every month.
So, these are all the feature i implemented in the app and are working fine. I have been using this app since last one month or so and based on any new requirements i feel like adding, i keep updating the app. My main focus was to AI enable the app as much as possible to automate the manual intervention and also to make the app more intelligent.
As i have already mentioned, my aim was to create this app from scratch without writing a single line of code nor fixing any errors manually. So am i successful in this? I can say, i’m fairly successful but only thing is that, i took full control of the development and took the help of AI LLM’s for the code generation and assembled everything myself and made it work.
What are my observations using AI LLM for code generation?
- AI LLM tools are honestly much better than even experienced programmers in terms of code generation. They can understand even the complex problems much faster and generate the code very fast.
- They are trained on vast data and hence they have an advantage of quickly giving solutions to problems in comparison to the developers manually referring to the docs.
- They can quickly analyse the code and pin point untraceble errors by the human developers.
- They understand and analyse the error logs faster and better and can pin point the root cause of the errors and the relevant fix.
- They can improve the code/logic based on the issues reported.
- If used properly, they can do the best job of coding
However, there are lots of challenges also
- They are stateless and they don’t understand what you are planning to do unless you explicitly mention everything to them in your question.
- They work better for less complex solutions and as the complexity grows they sometime hallucinate.
- They work better if the user is smart and is capable of dictating the terms than allowing them to throw a random code.
- If you are a novice developer and trying to build a medium complextiy application, you will face challenges because, they keep updating the code they have given with new code, everytime there is some change in terms of fixes or feature additions. You end up screwing up the working code and have to struggle.
- There is one more situation like first they propose solution A and you said it doesn’t work, then they propose solution B, that doesn’t work, they come back again with solution A!. So the user of the system should be smarter enought to catch these details and take an appropriate decesion.
- As i mentioned, they are good at solving small problems, it is always good to go in an incemental way to get the code. If you want everything to happen in one shot, you will never be able to achieve it using AI tools.
- The user should have good expertise in what he is trying to do and give proper instructions to AI to get what he wants than giving full control to AI. This is something like say you are an experienced builder, you have good experience in constructing big buildings, apartmentsetc, you know everything like estimates, labour, schedule,material etc and you decide how your building should be in terms of all these parameters and only use people to follow your instructions. In the same lines, if you are an experienced developer and trying to use AI for code generation, you can get a better results than just asking AI to build a complex app by itself.
- It is always good to get the code generated in an incremental way and take the code snippets, analyse and assmeble.
So in my opinion, AI code generation is very good provided the user knows what he wants and is asking the right questions with proper details. The developer should take the ownership of using AI for getting the work done. One thing we should remember is that, AI is a tool and the user is the sole responsibility of how to get best out of them. AI can augument to the developement work but cannot act as a replacement.
I worked few hours in a day to do this app in an incremental way and hence didn’t feel like i spent any significant time doing this.
The other things i have noticed is that, while i am gaining more confidence on AI code generation, i am becoming too lazy even to look at the code, look at the error logs etc. I do only if the code doesn’t work or AI can’t fix the issues. Honestly the whole attitude of the developer in me is gradually changing! Now i really wonder about new folks with less or no development experience starting to use AI code generation, eventually if our dependency on these tools increases, we literally tend to forget basic coding and problem solving skills. Only time will tell the truth!
Here is the demo video.
